items.description: The European Central Bank was right to raise rates when it did last month, President Christine Lagarde told Les Echos.

items.description:

items.description: Confira com Daniel Carvalho os assuntos que foram destaque da semana no Brasil. Com edição de Patricia Xavier e produção de Gisele Fernandes.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Chip Stocks Off to Rough Start in Third Quarter With 2-Day Skid
items.description: Semiconductor stocks are starting the third quarter with their worst two-day selloff in nearly a month.

items.title: Blackstone-Backed Jersey Mike’s Files for IPO as Listings Jump
items.description: Jersey Mike’s Subs Inc., the sandwich chain backed by Blackstone Inc., filed for an initial public offering that would join a rebound in US listing activity.

items.title: EG’s Gas-Station Firm Cumberland Farms Files for US IPO
items.description: Cumberland Farms Ltd, the gas station and convenience store operator, filed for a US initial public offering, joining a rush of debuts that’s powered a record start to the year.

items.title: America Runs on Natural Gas: Fuel Set to Surpass Oil as US Top Energy Source by 2030
items.description: For 75 years, petroleum has been the energy source that has powered the US more than any other. That’s about to change.

items.title: US Hiring Slows Sharply, Curbing Job-Market Momentum
items.description:
US hiring slowed sharply in June even as the unemployment rate fell, curbing some of the budding momentum in job growth this year.

items.title: Quantum Computing Firm IQM Falls After Going Public Via SPAC
items.description: IQM shares slipped in their first day of trading after the Finnish quantum computer producer went public in New York via a merger with a black-check firm.

items.title: AI Debt Deluge Makes Credit Market Look Safer While Masking Risk
items.description: On paper, the US corporate bond market is getting safer as more high-rated tech companies sell debt. Many investors fear that safety will prove illusory.
